overall_assessment: Not structurally clean for round 01. Occurrence and account-profile cutover existed on happy paths, but legacy nullable occurrence, receiver-user fallback, and raw repository payload construction remained active.
recommended_path: Resolve occurrence identity as required across backend/feed/Flutter DTOs, require account-profile recipient identity for authenticated share materialization, and move invite transport payload construction to typed DAL request objects.

findings.title: Invite target pipeline still accepted nullable occurrence and event-level fallback
findings.rationale: Round 01 still allowed nullable occurrence IDs in Laravel feed projection and Flutter invite DTO/result decoding, leaving event-scoped grouping fallbacks in the launch path.
findings.suggested_action: Make occurrence identity required/non-empty in projection rebuild, feed payloads, DTOs, result models, decoder tests, and payload handling. Remove event group fallback.

findings.title: Authenticated share materialization still fell back to receiver_user_id identity
findings.rationale: Round 01 share materialization could persist and query authenticated receiver edges without receiver_account_profile_id, preserving two recipient identity models.
findings.suggested_action: Require or bootstrap a personal Account Profile for authenticated share materialization and use receiver_account_profile_id for persistence/query ownership. Keep receiver_user_id only as internal actor/audit metadata.

findings.title: Flutter invite repository still owned raw transport payload maps
findings.rationale: Round 01 built share, direct invite, and contact import raw maps in the repository while the backend contract accepted Map payloads, bypassing the DAL/request boundary.
findings.suggested_action: Introduce typed DAL/backend request DTOs and assemble raw target_ref, recipient, and contact maps only at the backend adapter boundary.
